sections.title: 1. N+1 Query Patterns
2. Unnecessary Re-renders (Svelte)
sections.content:
The provided context is mostly server-side code. No Svelte component files were included, so no reactive re-render issues are visible in this context. The `datetime.ts` helpers (`formatDate`, `formatTime`, `formatDateTime`) are pure functions and safe from reactive loops.

One pattern to watch for when reviewing components: if a clock component calls `formatDateTime` inside a reactive statement tied to a `setInterval`, ensure the interval is cleared in `onDestroy` (see memory leaks below).

---

sections.title: 3. Caching Opportunities
4. Memory Leaks
5. Redundant Computations
sections.content:
**`src/lib/search.ts` — `new URL()` in `formatDisplayUrl` called per result render**

This is fine for infrequent calls, but if it's called in a list render loop (e.g., 10 results), consider that each `new URL()` allocates. No fix needed unless profiling confirms it's hot.

**`src/lib/bangs.ts` — `BANGS` object is already a module-level constant** — correct, no redundant work.

**`src/lib/server/search.ts` — `makeCacheKey` uses `JSON.stringify` with object literal**

```typescript
return JSON.stringify({ query, tab, safesearch, offset, freshness: freshness ?? '', ... });
```

`JSON.stringify` on every cache lookup is cheap but allocates a temporary object. A template literal key would be faster and allocation-free:

```typescript
return `${query}|${tab}|${safesearch}|${offset}|${freshness ?? ''}|${country ?? ''}|${+filterAds}|${+blockAds}|${+blockTrackers}|${count}`;
```

**`src/lib/server/altcha.ts` — `hmacHex` recreates HMAC on every call**

```typescript
function hmacHex(input: string): string {
    return crypto.createHmac('sha256', getSecret()).update(input).digest('hex');
}
```

`crypto.createHmac` is not expensive per se, but creating a new HMAC object on every challenge creation and verification is avoidable if throughput becomes a concern — Node's `crypto.createHmac` is already fairly optimized, so this is low priority.

---

sections.title: Priority Summary
sections.content:
| Issue | Severity | File |
|---|---|---|
| `usedSignatures` unbounded growth | High | `altcha.ts` |
| `rateLimitBuckets` never pruned | High | `search.ts` |
| `createAdminClient()` per plan lookup | Medium | `plan.ts` |
| `getSecret()` reads env each call | Low | `altcha.ts` |
| LRU cache eviction incorrect | Low | `ai.ts`, `search.ts` |
| `prune()` not called on read paths | Medium | `security.ts` |
| `makeCacheKey` JSON.stringify | Low | `search.ts` |

codeBlocks.code:

    const secret = env.ALTCHA_SECRET?.trim();  // called per verifySolution/createChallenge

const clients = new Map<string, Entry>();
function prune(now: number): void { /* only called from flagSuspicious */ }
return JSON.stringify({ query, tab, safesearch, offset, freshness: freshness ?? '', ... });
